title: Two Drugs Help Fix Fatty Liver in Animals
summary: Fatty liver disease is a common problem where too much fat builds up in the liver, which can harm the liver and heart. Scientists from the University of Barcelona found that two medicines, pemafibrate and telmisartan, can reduce liver fat in animals like rats and zebrafish. Using both drugs together worked well and might also help the heart. These drugs are already used for other health issues, so they could be safe and fast to try for fatty liver disease. The study aims to stop the disease early before it gets worse.

background_read: Fatty liver disease happens when too much fat collects in the liver, which is an organ that helps clean the blood. This can lead to liver damage and heart problems. Many people have this disease, but there aren't many treatments. Scientists are looking at old medicines that are already safe to see if they can help. In this study, they tested two drugs on animals to see if they reduce liver fat and protect the heart.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from the University of Barcelona
WHAT: Found that pemafibrate and telmisartan reduce liver fat in animals
WHERE: At the University of Barcelona and other research centers
WHY: To find a safe and effective treatment for fatty liver disease early on

perspectives.description: The study offers hope that existing drugs could help treat fatty liver disease safely.
